_doc: Plan of record for the full Caliber City. Built in spec coords (city center = world origin, +X east/+Y north, ground Z=0, 800cm grid). Build order is roads -> blockmask -> per-district fill -> coastline -> dressing, so no-overlap holds globally (buildings only fill road-bounded free blocks). Ocean Drive is generated by the slice generator (Scripts/gtc_citygen/slice_config.json) at spec coords (recenter OFF in city mode).

ocean_drive.note: South Beach barrier island; generated by the Ocean Drive slice generator at spec coords

river.notes: Miami-River-analog canal, spec width 6000-9000 cm (8000 chosen as the grid-snapped midpoint, 10 cells). Cut from (+150000,-10000) at the bay inland to (-40000,-25000), dividing Downtown (north) from Brickell (south). Crossed by 3 short arterial bridges at x~-5000/+35000/+75000.

roads.notes: All distances in cm, +X east / +Y north, center at world origin, ground Z=0; every number is taken from caliber_city_spec.json .roads / .boundary. Beltway = roads.ring_roads[0] (r=256000, w=4800, kind highway_beltway -> class highway, grade-separated viaduct Z~+1400). I-95 spine from roads.notes "near-straight expressway x~-140000 ... y[-300000..+300000], highway class"; x=-140000 (-175 cells) and y +/-300000 (+/-375 cells) are already on the 800 grid; width=highway carriageway 4800. Radial arteries = roads.radial_arteries (count 8 at 45deg, width 3200); inner_radius 64000 ("from core edge r~64000", 80 cells), outer 256000 reaches the beltway (320 cells); the east artery theta=0 becomes the MacArthur causeway. Ring road = roads.ring_roads[1] (r=120000=150 cells, w=3200, at-grade arterial with 8 signalized crossings). The 3 causeways are roads.causeways verbatim (carriageway 2400, deck on piers). River = boundary.notes Miami-River-analog canal (6000-9000 cm; 8000 = grid-snapped 10 cells), from (+150000,-10000) to (-40000,-25000), splitting Downtown/Brickell, crossed by 3 arterial bridges per roads.notes. Snap grid G=800; ROW half-widths from spec: highway 5000, causeway 3600, arterial 2400, ring/arterial 2400.

districts.name: Downtown/Brickell core
districts.notes: Downtown/Brickell high-rise core on the bay's west shore (Biscayne Bay to the east). Spec center_x/center_y=110000,10000 and radius=70000 snapped to the 800 grid -> center (109600,9600), half_extent 69600 per axis (disc bounding box). Local grid ROTATED 18deg from +X (Miami downtown grain) — the generator must rotate this district's local street lattice and all plots/footprints by rotation_deg in world space; spacing/columns are expressed in the rotated local frame. Roads: tight 800-grid blocks via local-class N-S and E-W streets at 8000 cm spacing (the minimum that satisfies D >= 2*H_row(1200) + 2*setback(400) + min_plot_depth(4800) = 8000), yielding a 4800 buildable block = one core plot module. plot_module_cm=6400 covers the spec's largest 6400x6400 footprint; smaller 3200/4800 footprints are subsets of their plots so no-overlap holds by construction. BUILD-TO-LINE: this is a downtown_highrise/party-wall core, so frontage setback is 0 (plots meet the sidewalk) and the two outer/front columns are party_wall=true (towers share Y walls inside a block, non-overlapping by construction). Columns outer->inner: (1) bay-front + corner HERO towers, frontage 6400 — place LI_Building_01/03 and the tallest SM_Skyscraper_* here; (2) skyscraper fill, frontage 4800; (3) inner BACKGROUND silhouette row (party_wall=false) for far skyline depth using SM_CityBeach_BackgroundBuilding_03/07. building_assets are EXACTLY this district's 8 spec paths, all verified on disk: LI_Building_01/03 are .umap Level Instances (is_li=true, spawn as LevelInstance actors); SM_Skyscraper_01-04 and SM_CityBeach_BackgroundBuilding_03/07 are static meshes (is_li=false). NOTE the spec also calls for splitting N(Downtown)/S(Brickell) by the Miami-River-analog canal cut at y~-12000..-25000, with Brickell (south) using slightly larger ~500 plaza setbacks and LI_Resort_* pools + a palm-lined N-S Brickell-Ave artery to the bayfront; those sub-zone refinements and resort/pool dressing are hand-finish and not encoded in these base columns. Coastline keep-out 16000 cm from the raw bay shoreline and the river-mouth bridge keep-outs still apply at the district edges.

districts.name: Wynwood
districts.notes: Wynwood arts/warehouse district, mainland NW of Downtown, outside-ish the ring road. Source-of-truth spec: name "Wynwood", center_x=40000, center_y=90000, radius_cm=48000, zone wynwood_midrise, density medium, building_footprint_cm=4000. SNAPPING: center.y snapped 90000->89600 (nearest 800 multiple; 90000 is not a multiple of 800). center.x=40000 and half_extent 48000x48000 (=radius) are already multiples of 800. ROTATION: 12 deg per spec "irregular angled local grid (rotate ~12deg)". ROADS: LOCAL-class grid only (spec: low local grid, no arteries inside the district; arteries/ring belong to the city layer). spacing 36800 cm both axes is a multiple of 800 and yields a buildable block of 36800 - 2*h_row(1200) - 2*setback(400) = 33600 cm, matching the spec's "big rectangular ~34000 cm blocks". Spacing rule satisfied with huge margin: D=36800 >= 2*1200 + 2*400 + min_plot_depth(4800) = 8000. Generator should add generous mid-block alleys (alley class, BackStreets/SM_BackStreet_01_8m) inside these large blocks per spec ("generous mid-block alleys"). COLUMNS outer->inner: two outer background-warehouse rows (cheap SM_CityBeach_BackgroundBuilding silhouettes, blank walls for murals, large 8000/6400 frontage warehouse lots), then two inner midrise rows of the hip converted-warehouse LI_Building_09/10/11 at 4800/4000 frontage. NOT party-wall anywhere (Wynwood is wide-footprint detached warehouse lots, not a continuous storefront wall; party-wall belongs to Downtown/South Beach/Little Havana). Frontages 4000-8000 per spec "larger plots (4000-8000 frontage)". plot_module_cm=4000 = spec building_footprint_cm; footprints range 2400x3200 to 4000x4800 (subset of plot). FRONTAGE SETBACK: 200 cm per spec (FS=200 for Wynwood/Havana) — applied by the solver as the build-to setback inside the plot, separate from the column frontage_cm (plot width). Low-rise 1-2 story. Set-dressing accent: SM_JacarandaTree, plus mural facades hand-finished with Architecture/SM_Base_Wall_* shutter modules (asset gap, not part of building_assets). ASSETS: all 7 building_assets are exactly the spec's Wynwood list, each verified on disk (LI_Building_09/10/11 as .umap level instances -> is_li=true spawned as LevelInstance actors; SM_CityBeach_BackgroundBuilding_01/04/05/06 as .uasset static meshes -> is_li=false). No skyscraper/resort assets here (those are Downtown/South Beach/Port). Coastline keep-out and city-layer roads (ring/arteries) handled by the global generator, not this district config.

districts.name: Little Havana
districts.notes: Little Havana, mainland SW (zone=lowrise_residential, density=high). center snapped from spec (10000,-80000) to (9600,-80000) on the 800 grid; half_extent 60000x60000 from spec radius 60000 (=75 grid cells). rotation_deg=0: regular fine E-W/N-S grid, NO district rotation (per task: Little Havana/suburban/port are 0deg; Downtown ~18, Wynwood ~12). Road grid is all LOCAL class (h_row=1200) at spacing 8000 in both axes — the spec minimum D >= 2*H_row(1200) + 2*setback(400) + min_plot_depth(4800) = 8000, giving a 4800 buildable block = one core plot module; tightest legal spacing for the high lot count / high density this district calls for. plot_module_cm=2400 = spec building_footprint_cm; small lots, footprints range 1600x2000..2400x2800. Columns outer->inner: (1) party_wall=true, frontage 0 — the continuous build-to-line storefront/rowhouse street wall along the Calle-Ocho-analog E-W arterial at y~-80000 (party-wall gap 0 inside the block by construction); (2) non-party residential row, frontage 400 (spec setback 200-400, upper bound for a setback porch line), inter-plot gap >=200; (3) cheap background-silhouette back row, frontage 200, non-party fill. Building palette = ONLY this district's spec building_assets, all verified on disk: LI_Building_14/15/16 are .umap Level Instances (is_li=true) used as the midrise shopfront/rowhouse heroes incl. the party-wall row; SM_CityBeach_BackgroundBuilding_02/05/06 are static meshes (is_li=false) for cheap back-row/far fill. Signs SM_CafeBar_Sign_01 + SM_HotlineBurger_Sign01 (is_li=false) are facade dressing (role=sign), not plot-filling buildings — generator should attach to storefront facades / furniture lane, not allocate plots to them. Hand-finish: add awnings, SM_OpenNeonSign, Jacaranda+palm street trees, SM_Bench, SM_WoodPlanter in the 150cm furniture lane (props not in this district's building_assets list so omitted here). All distances cm, every value a multiple of 800 except the intentional frontage setbacks (0/400/200) and side/rear insets handled by the solver.

districts.name: Port / Marina
districts.notes: Port/Marina at the south end of Biscayne Bay, mainland east shore; spec center (150000,-120000) snapped to (150400,-120000) on the 800 grid (x rounded up from 187.5 modules; y already on grid). half_extent 55200 = spec radius 55000 snapped up to 800 (district bbox half-extent). rotation_deg=0 per the port/suburban grain rule (no rotated grid). Low-density industrial/maritime, so the operative road tier is local at spacing 11200 (NS and EW): satisfies D >= 2*H_row(1200) + 2*SB(400) + min_plot_depth, leaving an 8000 cm buildable block that holds one big 6400-deep port plot plus the 800 perimeter inset and side/rear insets. plot_module_cm=6400 = spec building_footprint_cm (big open lots, footprints 4800x6400+). No party walls anywhere (industrial spacing, generous truck aprons / quay aprons / parking circulation). Columns run outer->inner toward the dredged basin: (1) background = cargo back-of-house sheds and far silhouette fill (SM_CityBeach_BackgroundBuilding_03/07), frontage 1600 for truck aprons; (2) resort = cruise-terminal LONG SHEDS reusing LI_Resort_01/03 as big sheds along the quay, frontage 800; (3) background = inland cargo/utility fill, frontage 1600; (4) resort = small-craft marina low residential / clubhouse on the calmer bay edge, frontage 2400 for generous low-density lots. GENERATOR-CRITICAL: the dredged rectangular PORT BASIN is water from (+150000,-150000) to (+215000,-95000) and must be carved into the WATER mask before block subdivision; quay walls sit on the 800 grid (dress with Boulders/SM_Boulder01-03). Small-craft marina slips near (+160000,-95000) on the calm bay edge. Keep the high-clearance bridge over the river mouth at (+148000,-12000) plus the south/port causeway as the only land connections; honor the standard 6000 cm bridge-deck and 16000 cm coastline keep-outs. SM_Carpark_DoorRamp is NOT a building-pool member — it is the explicit parking-deck approach-ramp prop (role parking_deck_ramp) the generator should place at parking-deck plots, not assign as a facade. ASSET GAP (hand-finish): no dock/crane/boat/cruise-ship mesh in the kit; sheds are LI_Resort_* placeholders.

districts.name: Outer suburban ring
districts.notes: Annular fill, NOT a disc: occupies the band between the ring road (r~120000) and the beltway (r~256000) on the west/north/south arcs wherever no other district reaches; center (-180000,40000) is only the western fill centroid, so the generator must clip plots to (BLOCKMASK-free land between those two radii) and not naively stamp a 240000x240000 box. center and half_extent already snap to 800 (center -225,50 cells; half_extent 150 cells). rotation 0 (suburban grain, axis-aligned E-W/N-S). Local-class streets only, but lay them as LOOSE CURVING polylines (SM_Street_Curve_01, SM_Street_Bend_60_01) drifting +/-1 cell every ~16000 for organic suburban feel rather than a rigid mesh. Spacing 9600 (multiple of 800) satisfies D >= 2*H_row(1200) + 2*SB(400) + min_plot_depth: 9600 - 2400 - 800 = 6400 buildable block, ample for the deep low-density lots. Big lots, low-rise residential, density ~20/km2 (lowest in the city). Two non-party-wall column rows between parallel locals: outer 'background' row uses SM_CityBeach_BackgroundBuilding_02/05 cheap silhouettes for far fill, inner 'midrise' row uses LI_Building_24/25/26/27 (the only LI buildings the spec assigns here). frontage_cm 600 per spec setback (frontage is an off-grid setback toward the fronting road, mirroring slice_config's 600/200/160 examples; all OTHER distances are 800-snapped). Per-spec inter-plot gap >=400 (non-party), side/rear inset >=100, so min_building_gap 400 holds. plot_module 3200 (multiple of 800) fits two ~2800 lots per 6400 block face with the 800 perimeter inset; footprint hint ~2000x2400. Dress with Jacarandas + palms in the furniture lane. Honor coastline keep-out (no plot within 16000 of shoreline) on the west/north arcs and the beltway cloverleaf keep-outs.
